parts.title: Principals
prompts: Create an agenda for a 60-minute staff meeting at a [primary/secondary school] focused on improving [specific area, e.g. student engagement or work environment], including three discussion points and a short group activity.
Give me five strategies for strengthening collaboration among teachers at a [grade level] school, with concrete examples of how they can be implemented over a school year.
Write a short welcome letter to parents ahead of a new school year at a [grade level] school, highlighting [the school's vision, values, and two new initiatives for the year].
Suggest three practical ways to integrate [specific focus, e.g. sustainability or digital literacy] into the school's overall operations at [grade level], including a simple evaluation method.
Create a list of five key questions for a survey to gather feedback from teachers and staff at a [grade level] school on [specific topic, e.g. workload or resources].
Give me an example of an [annual plan] for improving students' [specific skill, e.g. reading or problem-solving] at a [grade level] school, with goals, activities, and follow-up checkpoints.
Suggest three ways to recognise and celebrate teachers' contributions at a [grade level] school over a school year, including both formal and informal methods.
Write a short guide for running a 30-minute workshop for teachers at a [grade level] school on [specific topic, e.g. conflict management or inclusion], with a practical example.
Give me five ideas for involving students and parents in shaping the school's [specific project, e.g. anti-bullying work or a school garden] at a [grade level] school.
Create a checklist of five priorities for a principal to focus on during the first two weeks of a new school year at a [grade level] school, including a brief rationale for each point.

Create a plan for a 90-minute professional development day for teachers at a [grade level] school on the theme of [specific topic, e.g. digital tools or student wellbeing], including two workshops and a follow-up activity.
Give me five ideas for reducing stress and preventing burnout among staff at a [grade level] school during an intensive school year.
Write a short speech (2–3 minutes) to students at a [grade level] school to inspire them at the start of a new project or theme, e.g. [specific theme].
Suggest three ways to collaborate with the local community to strengthen resources or opportunities for a [grade level] school, with a concrete example for each.
Create a list of five discussion points for a meeting with the school leadership team on how to improve [specific area, e.g. safety or inclusion] at a [grade level] school.
Give me an example of a budget prioritisation for a [grade level] school that balances [specific needs, e.g. teaching materials and staffing], with a brief rationale.
Suggest three methods for collecting anonymous feedback from students at a [grade level] school on [specific topic, e.g. school environment or teaching], including follow-up steps.
Write a template for a weekly newsletter to staff at a [grade level] school with sections for updates, encouragement, and a reflection question.
Give me five ideas for increasing parental involvement in school activities at a [grade level] school, focusing on both in-person and digital opportunities.
Create a step-by-step guide for managing a conflict situation between two teachers at a [grade level] school, including three suggested mediation methods.
Suggest three initiatives to promote diversity and inclusion at a [grade level] school, with a short description of how each can be introduced.
Write a short evaluation plan for measuring the success of a new school programme (e.g. [specific programme]) at a [grade level] school, with three measurable indicators.
Give me five ways to use data (e.g. [attendance statistics or test results]) to make improvement decisions at a [grade level] school, with an example for each.
Create a list of three short activities to strengthen school identity or pride among students and staff at a [grade level] school.

Suggest a strategy for introducing and implementing a new routine (e.g. [specific routine, e.g. morning assembly or safety procedure]) at a [grade level] school, including a communication plan and a realistic timeline.
Create a plan for a theme day at a [grade level] school focused on [specific theme, e.g. environment or health], including three activities and a schedule.
Give me five strategies for managing a sudden budget cut at a [grade level] school without negatively affecting core operations.
Write a short message to parents at a [grade level] school explaining a new policy (e.g. [specific policy]) in a clear and positive way.
Suggest three ways to integrate student-led initiatives at a [grade level] school to promote [specific goal, e.g. responsibility or creativity].
Create a list of five questions to ask during an interview with a prospective teacher for a [grade level] school, focusing on [specific priority, e.g. collaboration or inclusion].
Give me an example of a crisis preparedness plan for a [grade level] school in the event of [specific situation, e.g. power outage or emergency], with three concrete steps.
Suggest five ways to use the school's physical environment (e.g. corridors, outdoor areas) to enhance learning at a [grade level] school.
Write a template for a monthly newsletter to students' families at a [grade level] school, with sections for news, upcoming events, and a call to engagement.
Give me fifteen ideas for strengthening the relationship between the school and local businesses or organisations at a [grade level] school.
Create a checklist of five points for evaluating how well a [grade level] school lives up to its vision and core values.
Suggest ten different ways to support new teachers during their first term at a [grade level] school, including a mentoring proposal and how it could work in practice.
Write a short encouraging message to staff at a [grade level] school during a busy period (e.g. [specific time of year]), with a concrete expression of thanks for their efforts.
Give me five ideas for reducing conflict between students at a [grade level] school through preventive work and positive routines.
Create a simple annual plan for introducing a new focus area (e.g. [specific focus]) at a [grade level] school, with three milestones.
Suggest three ways to use technology to streamline administrative tasks at a [grade level] school, with an example for each.
Write a list of five discussion questions for a meeting with the parent council at a [grade level] school on [specific topic, e.g. school meals or homework].
Give me an example of a student survey for a [grade level] school to measure wellbeing, with three short questions and an open-ended section.
Suggest three ways to celebrate the school's achievements (e.g. [specific achievement]) at a [grade level] school, both internally and publicly.
Create a strategy for managing an increase in student absence at a [grade level] school, including communication and follow-up.
Give me five ideas for strengthening the school's reputation in the local community at a [grade level] school, focusing on visibility and engagement.
Write a short guide for conducting a 20-minute reflection with staff at a [grade level] school on [specific topic, e.g. term goals].
Suggest three ways to include the student perspective in decision-making at a [grade level] school, with an example of a student council activity.
Create a list of five resources (e.g. books, websites) for principals at a [grade level] school to develop [specific skill, e.g. leadership or conflict management].
Give me an example of a simple routine for following up on student and staff wellbeing at a [grade level] school each month.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with other schools in the area for a [grade level] school, including a joint project proposal.
Suggest five effective methods for me as a principal to build a strong working relationship with union representatives at a [grade level] school in Sweden.
Create a plan for a 45-minute brainstorming session with staff at a [grade level] school to address [specific problem, e.g. declining student motivation], including three facilitation questions.
Write a short proposal for a pilot project to test [specific change, e.g. flexible break times] at a [grade level] school, including goals and an evaluation method.
Suggest three ways to use school data (e.g. grades or attendance) to identify and support at-risk students at a [grade level] school.
Create a list of five short, informal activities for building relationships between the principal and students at a [grade level] school over a term.
Give me an example of a simple action plan for managing an increase in parent complaints at a [grade level] school, with three steps and a communication strategy.
Write a short reflection guide for principals at a [grade level] school with three questions to answer each quarter to evaluate their leadership.
Give me five ideas for bringing the school's core values to life in everyday practice at a [grade level] school, with a concrete example for each.

Create a strategy for introducing a new collaboration with an external partner (e.g. [specific organisation]) at a [grade level] school, including goals and first steps. Develop a strategy for improving our systematic quality assurance work at a [grade level] school, including goals and steps to get there. We currently have gaps in [specify areas with quality shortfalls]. Bonus prompt – Leadership Coach: Act as an expert in leadership. I am a principal who wants to develop my leadership approach with the teachers at my school.
How can I develop and improve my leadership? Ask me questions — one question per message — to find out where I currently stand and give me advice and tips.
How do I use the bonus prompt?
Type in the full prompt and answer the follow-up questions you receive from the chatbot. Remember not to include any sensitive information!
